nodes.label: Battery
nodes.notes: Group 34/78 minimum for full accessory load

nodes.label: Alternator
nodes.notes: Powermaster-based unit for Holley mid-mount. Self-exciting — no Holley 197-400 excite pigtail needed. Includes external ground lug and corrected 3in pulley ratio.

nodes.label: Starter
nodes.notes: Powermaster Starter for LS from Level 7 Motorsports. 2.5 kW / 3.4 hp motor. 4.4:1 gear reduction.

nodes.label: Holley ECM
nodes.notes: Terminator X Max. Requires external 2-bar MAP: GM 12592525 + adapter 558-416

nodes.label: LS3 Coils (x8)
nodes.notes: Factory LS3 coil packs — retained from crate engine

nodes.label: Injectors (x8)
nodes.notes: Size to be confirmed after blower selection

nodes.label: DBW Throttle
nodes.notes: Drive-by-wire TB. Pedal: GM 10379038

nodes.label: Cooling Fan
nodes.notes: ECM-controlled via PWM output. Fan-on threshold set in Holley software

nodes.label: A/C Compressor
nodes.notes: Vintage Air compressor — coordinate mount with blower accessory drive

nodes.label: Wideband O2
nodes.notes: Included with Holley 550-931 kit

nodes.label: MAP Sensor
nodes.notes: External 2-bar MAP required for boost. Adapter: Holley 558-416

nodes.label: Fuel Pressure Sensor
nodes.notes: 100 PSI stainless sensor. Mounts in 1/8 NPT port on Holley 12-876 regulator. Wired to Terminator X Max harness fuel pressure input. Data broadcast via CAN to Dakota HDX through BIM-EFI-1.

nodes.label: Height Sensor LF
nodes.notes: TruPosition rotary sensor, front left corner. Wired via Accuair plug-and-play harness to e+ ECU in rear node. Route along driver frame rail keeping separate from fuel and ignition wiring.

nodes.label: Height Sensor RF
nodes.notes: TruPosition rotary sensor, front right corner. Wired via Accuair plug-and-play harness to e+ ECU in rear node. Route along passenger frame rail.

nodes.label: AAW Fuse Panel
nodes.notes: Classic Update Series — First Design. Add 150A mega fuse on charge cable

nodes.label: Ignition Switch
nodes.notes: Routes through AAW harness — verify neutral safety wiring for T56

nodes.label: Dakota HDX
nodes.notes: CAN bus via BIM-EFI-1. Set Holley CAN to Racepak in software

nodes.label: BIM-EFI-1
nodes.notes: Replaces BIM-01-2-HLLY. Bluetooth config via Dakota Digital app. Bridges Holley ECM CAN data to HDX cluster. Two aux inputs used for SEN-04-5 trans and diff temp sensors.

nodes.label: BIM-04-2 Temps
nodes.notes: 4x temp inputs: oil, coolant, trans, diff. Sensor: SEN-04-5 x4

nodes.label: TouchPad+
nodes.notes: Magnetic wired physical controller. USB cable runs to e+ ECU in rear node — this is the single cab penetration for the entire Accuair system.

nodes.label: Vintage Air HVAC
nodes.notes: ProLine 3-knob horizontal control. Requires Gen IV or Gen 5 evaporator — confirm ECU pairing with Vintage Air tech before ordering evaporator.

nodes.label: Kenwood DMX908S
nodes.notes: Wireless CarPlay/Android Auto. 6-ch 5V preouts. USB-C. HDMI input.

nodes.label: Ground Block (Marine)
nodes.notes: Star ground point — engine to frame, frame to body, battery negative. Critical for Dakota HDX and ECM sensor accuracy. All sensor grounds terminate here before single heavy run to battery negative.

nodes.label: Power Block (Marine)
nodes.notes: Positive distribution block. Feeds fuse panel, ECM power feed, and accessory circuits from battery via 4/0 AWG main feed. Also feeds Accuair compressor relays directly — do not route 444C compressor loads through fuse panel.

nodes.label: T56 Service Disconnect
nodes.notes: Mid-vehicle bulkhead connector at trans crossmember. Allows trans removal without cutting cab wiring. VSS, reverse signal, lockout solenoid, neutral safety pass through here.

nodes.label: Power Windows
nodes.notes: Tier 2 accessory — planned

nodes.label: Heated Seats
nodes.notes: 4-piece 12V carbon fiber kit, 27W per pad, 3-level control with built-in thermostats. Driver and passenger zones controlled separately via Ring Brothers On/On/On switches.

nodes.label: Alarm System
nodes.notes: Tier 2 accessory — planned

nodes.label: Backup Camera
nodes.notes: Tier 2 — integrate with Kenwood DMX908S rear camera input

nodes.label: Fuel Relay
nodes.notes: ECM triggers relay coil. Relay supplies power to C105 controller. Mounted on C105 mounting plate at frame rail near tank.

nodes.label: DW C105 Controller
nodes.notes: Dual speed controller. 68% low / 100% high duty cycle. ECM ground-side trigger switches low/high speed. Mounted on custom aluminum plate at frame rail near tank.

nodes.label: DW440 Fuel Pump
nodes.notes: 440lph brushless. Custom hat on Boyd 6in cleanout plate. 10 AWG wiring. Racetronix BCWS-001 4-wire bulkhead connector through hat.

nodes.label: Boyd Fuel Sender
nodes.notes: Updated Boyd sender for 10in tank depth. Wired separately from pump — dedicated path to gauge.

nodes.label: Accuair e+ ECU
nodes.notes: Brain of the Accuair system. Bluetooth 5.0 for smartphone control. RGB backlighting. Processes all height sensor data and controls VU4 manifold and compressors. Mounts in rear node near air tank. Switched IGN feed from fuse panel via rear trunk. Single USB cable to TouchPad+ in cab.

nodes.label: VU4 Manifold
nodes.notes: 4-corner electric solenoid valve block. Controls directional air flow to/from each air spring independently. Controlled by signal from e+ ECU. Dedicated fused circuit from fuse panel via rear trunk.

nodes.label: ENDO Air Tank
nodes.notes: Air reservoir. Installed unit is ENDO-CVT (discontinued — integrated compressor design). Replacement spec: ENDO tank + dual Viair 444C external compressors. Tank size: confirm 3 or 5 gallon based on available space in bed area.

nodes.label: Compressor Relay 1
nodes.notes: Dedicated relay for Viair 444C compressor #1. Fed direct from power block — do not route through fuse panel. ECU triggers relay coil. 25A fuse on output feed.

nodes.label: Compressor Relay 2
nodes.notes: Dedicated relay for Viair 444C compressor #2. Fed direct from power block — do not route through fuse panel. ECU triggers relay coil. 25A fuse on output feed.

nodes.label: 444C Compressor 1
nodes.notes: Primary air compressor. 200 PSI max, 100% duty cycle at 100 PSI. 23A draw at max load. Fed via dedicated relay from power block. 10 AWG minimum wiring. Note: installed unit is ENDO-CVT with integrated compressor — 444C is replacement/upgrade spec.

nodes.label: 444C Compressor 2
nodes.notes: Secondary air compressor. Mirrors Compressor 1 spec. Dual compressor setup reduces fill time and provides redundancy. Fed via dedicated relay from power block. 10 AWG minimum wiring.

nodes.label: Air Pressure Sensor
nodes.notes: Monitors air tank PSI. Reports to e+ ECU to control compressor cycling. Plug-and-play connection to ECU harness.

nodes.label: Height Sensor LR
nodes.notes: TruPosition rotary sensor, rear left corner. Wired via Accuair plug-and-play harness to e+ ECU. Short local run — ECU mounted in same rear node.

nodes.label: Height Sensor RR
nodes.notes: TruPosition rotary sensor, rear right corner. Wired via Accuair plug-and-play harness to e+ ECU. Short local run — ECU mounted in same rear node.

nodes.label: Tail/Brake Lights
nodes.notes: Rear trunk loom. LED recommended for reduced load

nodes.label: E-Stopp Module
nodes.notes: Electronic parking brake module. Under-seat panel — passenger side. Power isolated from Holley ECU path via dedicated DT 2-way disconnect at battery junction and DT 4-way disconnect at panel edge. Button wire routes to dash or console — location TBD.

nodes.label: E-Stopp Power Disconnect
nodes.notes: Service disconnect at battery junction — de-powers E-Stopp module without disturbing panel wiring. DT 2-way — power and ground.

nodes.label: E-Stopp Module Disconnect
nodes.notes: Service disconnect at panel — allows E-Stopp module removal without disturbing panel wiring. DT 4-way — power, ground, button signal, button ground.

edges.notes: Power feed to E-Stopp power disconnect at battery junction — keep isolated from ECU power path
Power run from junction disconnect to module disconnect on panel
Final power feed into E-Stopp module from panel disconnect
Button signal through module disconnect — routes forward to console or dash button
